prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT small object standing FREE on a small flat patch of dark near-black volcanic rock — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a charred tree stump 2) a black rock with a sulphur crust 3) a cooling lava rock with glowing cracks 4) a pile of dark volcanic rocks 5) a small crater rim of black rock 6) a scorched animal skull 7) a cluster of obsidian shards 8) a chunk of black obsidian 9) a small steaming vent 10) a small black crystal cluster. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
